title: Clausena nana
intro: Clausena nana, also known as the small citron or Thai small citron, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Clausena nana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit potential health benefits. Clausena nana is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Clausena nana is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. This family is further divided into several genera, and Clausena nana belongs to the genus Clausena. The genus Clausena comprises a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions, and they are commonly known as poovan or wild curry leaf. Clausena nana is a small shrub or tree species, and its classification reflects its close relationship to other plants in the Rutaceae family.
